definition: The yield curve remains the most reliable recession/cycle leading indicator but requires term premium decomposition. Pure inversion is insufficient in the QT era — inversion from expected-rate compression signals recession while inversion from term premium compression may not. ACM and Kim-Wright models diverge by 30-50bp.
evidence: Historical support for steepening trap in 1990, 2001, 2007
Current 2s10s at +20-40bp with Tier 2b signpost active
30-50bp divergence between ACM and Kim-Wright term premium models
Markets celebrate un-inversion as all-clear but recession arrives during steepening
open_questions: Whether 30-50bp model divergence introduces meaningful uncertainty for actionable signals
Whether QT has permanently altered the term premium signal
How to distinguish expected-rate vs term-premium driven inversions in real time
